mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate held near historic lows keeps the equity discount rate compressed relative to earnings yield, sustaining a persistent tailwind for equity valuations. Low and stable FEDFUNDS readings are associated with positive forward S&P 500 returns over the following month.
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS level and forward 20d returns falls below 0.03 over any rolling 3-year window, or periods of low FEDFUNDS show no statistically significant excess return versus periods of high FEDFUNDS (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the unemployment rate of more than 0.2 percentage points over three consecutive monthly readings signals deteriorating labor market conditions that precede consumption pullbacks and earnings downgrades, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Triggered instances of UNRATE increases show positive median 20-day forward returns in more than 55% of cases, or the effect is not significant at p > 0.10 in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: When nonfarm payroll growth decelerates while the federal funds rate remains elevated relative to its recent trailing average, the joint signal indicates a policy-tightening-into-slowdown regime that compresses forward equity risk premia, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter. Neither PAYEMS deceleration nor FEDFUNDS level alone is sufficient; the effect requires their joint occurrence.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month annualized growth decelerates below prior 6-month average AND FEDFUNDS above its trailing 12-month median
failure_condition: Joint-trigger instances fail to produce negative median 60-day forward returns relative to baseline, or the interaction term adds no incremental Rank IC versus single-series PAYEMS or FEDFUNDS models over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansion regime, a decline in housing starts signals weakening forward-looking demand for durable goods and construction activity, which historically leads broader economic slowdowns and depresses equity returns over the following quarter. The effect is regime-specific because early-cycle housing dips often reflect supply constraints rather than demand weakness.
failure_condition: HOUST declines in late-cycle periods are not followed by below-median 60-day forward SPX returns, or the sign of the relationship flips to positive with statistical significance (p < 0.05) in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained monthly increases in industrial production reflect expanding manufacturing capacity utilization and corporate output, which flows through to earnings growth and supports positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC between INDPRO trailing 3-month growth and forward 20d returns is below 0.03 across any 2-year rolling window, or negative INDPRO growth periods do not show worse forward returns than positive growth periods.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp deceleration in retail sales growth below a defined threshold within a single month signals an abrupt consumer demand shock that markets have not yet priced, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the following trading week as the news is digested.
failure_condition: Triggered RSAFS contraction events are followed by positive median 5-day forward returns in more than 55% of instances, or the effect disappears (p > 0.10) once controlling for contemporaneous market volatility.

mechanism_narrative: When headline CPI accelerates while the federal funds rate remains near zero, the combination implies a deeply negative real policy rate that raises the probability of future rapid tightening, introducing forward-looking equity risk premium expansion and negative S&P 500 returns over the next month. Neither elevated CPI nor a low FEDFUNDS level alone captures the regime; their joint occurrence signals the real-rate distortion.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL year-over-year change exceeds 2.5% AND FEDFUNDS below 0.5%
failure_condition: Joint-trigger periods show no significant negative 20-day forward return versus non-triggered periods (p > 0.10), or the interaction model underperforms single-series CPIAUCSL-only model in out-of-sample Rank IC.

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary tightening cycle, a deceleration in nonfarm payroll growth amplifies downside risk to equities because markets interpret weakening labor demand as confirmation that tightening is already restricting real activity, causing pronounced negative S&P 500 returns over the following quarter. The same payroll deceleration in an easing cycle is not expected to carry the same negative signal, since it would instead be read as justification for further easing.
failure_condition: PAYEMS deceleration during tightening regimes fails to show significantly more negative 60-day forward returns than PAYEMS deceleration during easing regimes (difference-in-means p > 0.10), rejecting regime conditionality.
